t1: This was step 1 of a two-step boundary search. We established residue 700 as the first-pass cut point based on the pLDDT disorder dip at positions 685-694, and ran a ±50 sweep to verify it was locally optimal. Step 2 — pushing further past the LRR linker to residue 1075 — is validated in §8 (ThreeConstructs) and §9–§11 (Ultra-Mini gates).
t2: Result: Residues 700 and 720 are both optimal (pTM 0.86), while cutting at 650 or 680 includes the disorder dip (residues 685-694, pLDDT 31-39) and gives worse results. This confirms the pLDDT-based first-pass boundary. Pushing further to 1075 preserves fold quality (pTM 0.87, §8) and doubles AAV headroom.
t3: The 21-amino acid IgK signal peptide has no meaningful effect on the fold (pTM 0.86 → 0.85, within noise). This is expected: the SP is cleaved co-translationally in the ER and is not part of the mature protein. The same IgK-SP is used on Ultra-Mini (see §14).
t15: 7. Truncation boundary — step 1: sweep around residue 700 4 AF3 jobs
